title: Chunking Is a Design Decision, Not a Detail
concept: Framing chunking strategy as one of the highest-leverage decisions in a RAG system, not an afterthought.

exercises.prompt: In our last lesson, we saw how bad chunking corrupts meaning. What is a direct consequence of splitting a document mid-sentence?

options.text: The LLM will crash and throw a syntax error.
Critical context is broken, leading to poor retrieval accuracy.
The vector database will run out of storage space.

exercises.explain: When chunks are split mid-sentence or mid-table, the core context is lost, resulting in poor retrieval and incorrect answers from the LLM.

cards.title: The Invisible Bottleneck
cards.body: Many developers treat chunking like a simple file-splitting script, setting a random character limit and moving on. But your chunking strategy is actually the lens through which your LLM views your entire document library.

cards.title: Strategic Chunking
cards.body: Chunking strategy is the deliberate practice of defining how document boundaries, sizes, and overlaps are configured to optimize both search retrieval and LLM synthesis. It is a high-leverage architectural choice that dictates what information is visible to your model in a single glance.

cards.title: The Architectural Blueprint
cards.body: Think of chunking like cutting a blueprint into smaller pieces to share with workers. If you cut right through a critical diagram, the workers won't understand their tasks; if you cut neatly along room boundaries, everyone works efficiently.

cards.title: Context-Aware Medical Q&A
cards.body: A medical app chunks health manuals by entire medical procedures rather than fixed character counts. This prevents critical step-by-step instructions from being split in half, ensuring the LLM receives the full context for patient safety.

cards.title: The 'Infinite Context' Trap
cards.body: Many believe that because modern LLMs have massive context windows, we can just throw raw documents at them. In reality, retrieving oversized or poorly structured chunks wastes API tokens, introduces irrelevant noise, and dilutes the model's focus.

cards.title: Chunking Strategies Compared
cards.body: Compare how different chunking strategies affect the context passed to your LLM.

left.text: Preserves complete paragraphs, tables, and logical sections together.

right.text: Cuts off mid-sentence, splitting related facts across different blocks.

exercises.prompt: When designing a RAG system, how should you determine the optimal chunk size and strategy?

options.text: Always use exactly 100 characters to keep things fast.
Let the database choose a random size automatically.
Align the chunk size with your specific user queries and LLM task requirements.
Always use the largest possible chunk that fits the LLM window.

exercises.explain: The optimal chunk size depends entirely on what your application needs to do. Short search queries require precise, granular chunks, while complex summaries need broader, larger context blocks.

exercises.prompt: True or False: A highly advanced LLM can easily fix and understand a chunk that was poorly cut in half during retrieval.
exercises.explain: Poor chunking ruins retrieval quality, and no LLM—no matter how advanced—can generate a correct answer if the retrieved context is missing or corrupted.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the missing phrase to describe the impact of chunking on a RAG system:
exercises.sentence: Choosing your chunking strategy is a high-leverage decision that dictates RAG success.
exercises.explain: Because chunking directly determines the quality of the information retrieved by your system, it is a high-leverage decision that shapes the success of the entire RAG application.

exercises.prompt: Which of these conversations sounds like an LLM Engineer discussing chunking as a key design decision?

options.text: 'I am going to write a script to rename all our PDF files to lowercase so they load faster.'
'We should evaluate if semantic-based chunking yields better retrieval accuracy than fixed-size chunking for our legal contracts.'
'I need to upgrade the database software tonight to get the latest security patches.'

exercises.explain: The second option presents a detailed, strategic RAG architectural design decision, whereas the others focus on basic database administration or simple coding tasks.

exercises.prompt: Why should developers treat chunking as a core architectural design decision rather than a minor detail in a RAG pipeline?

options.text: Chunking is done after retrieval, so it only affects database storage costs.
Chunking dictates the boundaries of the context fed to the LLM, directly shaping its understanding.
It is a fully automated detail that never needs human evaluation or adjustment.
Chunking only matters if you are using an outdated LLM with small context windows.

exercises.explain: Chunking dictates what the LLM 'sees' as a single context unit. Because it determines whether critical relationships and complete thoughts are preserved together, it directly impacts the accuracy of the final generated answer, making it a primary design decision rather than a simple preprocessing step.
